title: Powering established SME growth: The economic footprint of Allica Bank's lending
gist: Oxford Economics' commissioned analysis for Allica Bank finds the bank's 2025 SME lending supported an £8.4bn GDP contribution, 118,000 jobs and £2.1bn in tax revenues, and underscores that established SMEs (5–249 employees) anchor private-sector employment far more outside London and the South East.

insights.claim: Oxford Economics estimates Allica Bank's 2025 SME lending supported an £8.4 billion contribution to UK GDP, 118,000 jobs, and £2.1 billion in tax revenues.
insights.speaker: Suki Masana
insights.so_what: Quantifies a specialist SME lender's macro footprint; a data point for sizing the established-SME credit market and Allica's role in it.
insights.quote: this lending supported a £8.4 billion contribution to UK GDP, 118,000 jobs, and £2.1 billion in tax revenues.
insights.locator: Our latest analysis for Allica Bank

insights.claim: Established SMEs with 5 to 249 employees account for more than a third of UK private-sector employment, rising to 44% of private-sector jobs in Wales, 39% in Scotland and the South West, 38% in the North East and North West, and nearly half in rural areas.
insights.speaker: Suki Masana
insights.so_what: Shows SME credit conditions disproportionately drive regional/rural UK employment; a transmission channel to watch for levelling-up and regional growth.
insights.quote: they provide 44% of private sector jobs in Wales, 39% in Scotland and the South West, and 38% in both the North East and North West. In rural areas, they support almost half of all private sector employment.
insights.locator: Established SMEs – businesses with 5 to 249 employees
